hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific TM5 interactions, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2's ECL3 pocket and reduce off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current design strategy is generating candidates with strong SSTR2 binding but insufficient structural quality and selectivity optimization. Tightening structural constraints and focusing mutagenesis on non-pharmacophore positions will improve both yield and selectivity.

proposed_changes.rationale: Reducing the clash tolerance will enforce better packing and reduce structural failures due to poor geometry.

Focusing design on non-pharmacophore positions known to contribute to off-target binding (SSTR3/SSTR5) will allow more targeted disruption of off-target interactions while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
